content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

January 8, 1985


Daniel L. Briggs
12600 Central Avenue
Chino, CA 91710

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-84-320

Dear Mr. Briggs:

You have requested our advice with regard to your reporting
requirements under the campaign provisions of the Political
Reform Act.1/ As we understand them the facts are that you
retained legal counsel to represent you with regard to a special
city council meeting dealing with your private practice as an
architect and contractor. At the time of the special council
meeting, you were a member of the city council.

ANALYSIS

The Commission has held that legal expenses which are
incurred in matters which relate to the officeholder's status or
duties as an officeholder should be reported as campaign
expenditures. Funds raised for such purposes are to be treated
as campaign contributions and are to be reported accordingly. I
enclose copies of advice letters which discuss the reasoning
more fully. Should you desire specific assistance in filling
out the campaign forms, please do not hesitate to contact
Ms. Helen Arriola in our Los Angeles Office at (213) 620-5196.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh
cc: Helen Arriola
Jimmy Gutierrez, City Attorney, Chino
Enclosures

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.

Daniel L. Briggs
12600 Central Avenue
Chino, CA 91710

DEC 17 | 27 PM 84

December 11, 1984

Ms. Barbara A. Milman, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
1100 "K" Street Building
Sacramento, CA 95814

Reference: Request for Opinion and Advise/Government Code Section
83114.

Dear Ms. Milman,

Enclosed please find a letter outlining specific circumstances of
which I wish your opinion and advise.

Your careful consideration at your earliest convenience would be
greatly appreciated.

Respectfully submitted,

Daniel L. Briggs

Daniel L. Briggs
12600 Central Avenue
Chino, CA 91710


December 11, 1984

Mr. Jimmy Guiterrez, City Attorney
City of Chino
12616 Central Avenue
Chino, CA 91710

Personal and Confidential.

Dear Jimmy,

During my service as a Councilman in the City of Chino certain individuals both publicly and privately made asertians as to my private practice as an Architect and General Contractor and created a political controversy which culiminated in a special council meeting to clear the matter.

As a result of this special council meeting I sought legal counsel and incurred substantial costs.

Please advise me as to my responsibility for reporting these expenses. In the eventuality that my campaign committee is required to report such expenses, do they qualify as appropriate expenditures as defined in section 82025 Chapter 2?

Respectfully submitted,

[signature]
Daniel L. Briggs

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441


December 28, 1984


Daniel L. Briggs
12600 Central Avenue
Chino, CA 91710

Re: A-84-320

Dear Mr. Briggs:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or unless more information is needed to answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 working days.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        General Counsel

BAM:plh

QUESTIONAIRE

1. What in your opinion are the greatest assets of our community? _________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________

2. What, in your opinion, are our greatest liabilities? ___________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________

3. Which of the following statements best indicate your feeling about growth in Stockton.
   A.______ Our city should not expand beyond it's present boundaries.
   B. ______ Our city should review it's present boundries and allow residential and commercial 
             development on an as needed basis.
   C. ______ Our city should be allowed to grow but only after services such as schools and city
             services are in place.
             Comments: ___________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________

4. What are some steps that you feel the city council should take to encourage business and industrial
expansion in Stockton? ________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________

5. Are you satisfied with Stockton's means of electing it's councilpersons by district? Yes ☐ No☐

6. Please indicate by number how you would prioritize city spending.

______ Parks                    ______ Refuse Pick Up            ______ Weed Abatement
______ Streets                  ______ Paramedic Services        ______ Library Services
______ Police                   ______ Traffic Controls          ______ Other
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________

Comments: __________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________________________

GENERAL FUND
1984 - 85 Budget by Function

COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT 3.89%
GENERAL GOVERNMENT (COUNCIL, CLERK,
MANAGER, FINANCE, ATTORNEY & PERSONNEL) 6.61%
NON-DEPARTMENTAL 2.78%

PARKS AND
RECREATION
10.59%

LIBRARY
7.12%

PUBLIC WORKS
14.64%

POLICE
30.44%

FIRE
23.21%

ANIMAL
REGULATION 0.72%

It is a most difficult and frustrating task to implement a multi-million dollar
city budget. As you are aware it is a time for setting priorities, for understanding restraint and limitations. One of the main reasons for this newsletter is
to solicit your advice through the enclosed questionnaire so that I can best
represent your interests in City Hall. As you know democracy is a complex
chain and the most vital link is that of communication between the elected
representatives and his or her constituency. In the past three years I have
held over a dozen town-hall meetings in the district in order to solicit advice
and counsel. I value your comments at any time.

Rand Rook

Home - 473-3112    City Hall - 944-8244

There are three recreational parks in my district . . . Cortez, Valverde, and Dentoni Park. How unfortunate it is that most families living near their neighborhood parks have been harassed by offensive young punks who, in many cases, do not live in the neighborhood. This city, in many cases, has been forced to close their restrooms in the parks, due to vandalism. I have consistantly opposed building new parks until we are able to patrol and upgrade our existing ones.

Mayor Ronk reviewing constituent correspondence with secretary Kathy Glick.

Our youths are, without a doubt, our most valuable assets. As a father, I join in your frustrations that there are no recreational facilities in our district. I hope that in the near future, our city will respond to this need by building a recreation center, possibly in one of our existing parks. This would allow safe and productive activities for our children.

Mayor Ronk with son Bryan

After several attempts in recent years, I am pleased to report that the city council has finally approved paramedic services in North East Stockton. Engine Company No. 11, which which is located near Swain Road and Tam O'Shanter, is now equipped and ready as a paramedic Engine Company.

Mayor Ronk discussing public safety issues with Senior Administrative Assistant George Moton.

sections.question_synthetic: Must a city council member report legal expenses incurred to defend against political controversy regarding his private practice as an architect and contractor, and if so, do these qualify as appropriate campaign expenditures under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Legal expenses related to matters concerning the officeholder's status or duties must be reported as campaign expenditures, and funds raised for such purposes must be treated as campaign contributions and reported accordingly.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document contains multiple embedded pieces: the incoming request letter (December 11, 1984), FPPC acknowledgment letter (December 28, 1984), and formal FPPC advice response (January 8, 1985). Last pages appear to be unrelated Stockton city materials (budget questionnaire and mayor's newsletter) and were excluded from analysis. Main Q/C sections extracted from appropriate FPPC response letter dated January 8, 1985.

embedding.summary: Daniel L. Briggs, a Chino city council member, sought FPPC advice regarding whether legal expenses incurred to defend against political controversy over his private architectural and contracting practice must be reported as campaign expenditures. The FPPC concluded that such legal expenses directly related to an officeholder's status or duties must be reported as campaign expenditures.
